swamps: BARNACLE GEESE. Wrecked ships covered with barnacles that hatch into geese. Children with small, sharp knives collecting the embryonic geese. They are valuable as delicacies, but you have to be small and nimble to crawl around the wrecks without cutting yourself on the shells.

BRENDAN’S ISLAND. Monastery of St. Brendan the Navigator, discoverer of the continent of far-away lands. The cheerful monks brew excellent beer and love to wrestle. St. Brendan’s grave is sited on a phantom island, just offshore, which only exists for people who are blind drunk. He was buried with a compass that always points to the heart’s desire of its bearer. It is guarded by an ancient dwarf with a quarterstaff, immensely strong and sullen, who will bash in the head of anyone who tries to take it. Also if you sober up while you’re on the island it vanishes and you fall into the sea.

EASTMARCH. Castle built on a tidal island in the middle of a marsh lake, only accessible at low tide or with a boat. The Lord despises women. He keeps marrying new brides, accusing them of unchastity and confining them to the cells beneath the castle, which flood at high tide. They drown when they get tired of paddling to keep their heads above water. The fish carry their bodies away to a sunken temple under the castle, where an enchantress places their bodies in a green glass cauldron full of bubbling black smoke and reanimates them as fishes. The fishwives are gilled, translucent, beautiful, equipped with needle-sharp teeth and claws. The enchantress is the same, only morbidly obese. She feasts on the flesh of who drowns and is trying to figure out how to raise the temple back to the surface.

BEACHED SERPENT. Beached, dying sea serpent. Fishermen and circling swamp gryphons (vulturous, oily, love games) sharing an uneasy truce - there’s enough meat here for everyone, though until it actually dies nobody dares get too close to it. Pearlescent scales are mildly valuable and scuffles are already breaking out over who should get to claim them.

RAIDED TOWN. Town on a flat tidal island, flooded, burning and ruined. The stone walls that were supposed to keep out the water at high tide have been broken by the orcs. A couple of terrified peasants have been left stranded on rooftops and attics. A handful of orcs patrol the submerged streets, looking for the last remaining scraps of loot or flesh. Their leader rides a massive wild boar and has a necklace of human ears.

2d4 short halflings squatting on a beach, roasting clams over a firepit. One is playing a flute. Empty sealskins lie on the sand nearby. If you steal their skins they’ll have to be your slaves, but the rest of their clan will try to rescue them. They are cunning and efficient rogues and they do not forget a grudge.

SEDGE NYMPHS. Village overrun with hungry, carnivorous toads. Most adults fled or dead, some children surviving in branches of tall tree. Close by, a stagnant lake - the nymphs who live in the slimy water have sent the plague of toads to punish a child who dared to steal marsh holy flowers.

SAFE HARBOUR. Fishing village on stilts. Pub called the Safe Harbour serves pale, salty marsh ale brewed from mushrooms. Effects sneak up on the drinker - five pints have almost no effect, six knock you out. The villagers have cut a secret deal with the orcs they set false lights to confuse travellers, and deliver them kidnapped, in exchange for being left alone. If the PCs get drunk and pass out they’ll wake up in the slave cages. A hedge wizard has been living for the last six months in a hut just outside town - he doesn’t drink alcohol and has no idea all this is going on. He’s studying the anaesthetic properties of the local fungus.

CRABS. Low, rocky island (if next to the sea, otherwise in the middle of a lake/river) covered with thousands of voracious black crabs. Green glass statue of a woman lying in a tidepool in the centre - heavy, fragile, beautiful, valuable. At high tide the whole island is covered and the statue is several feet down below the surface, with crabs swarming over it.

WINDMILLS. Windmills pumping the water out of a wide, shallow lake with Archimedean screws, turning it into arable land. Irrigation ditches lead to the river. One of the windmills is broken - it’s been sabotaged by kobolds, who still infest the machinery. A confused squire looking for his knight, who was last seen jousting the windmills.

SWORD NYMPH. A serene, quiet lake. If anyone approaches the lake a nymph will emerge, offer them a +1 sword and tell them they’re destined to become the rightful king of all Faerie. If a party approaches, the nymph will only offer the sword to the most charismatic person.

STUCK GIANT. Giant sunk waist-deep in mud. Has been stuck there for the last hundred years or so. Ravenously hungry - can’t stop himself from trying to grab and eat anyone who gets within arm’s reach. Happy to talk to anyone else, but will try to lure them closer. Promises treasure to anyone who can get him out.

BARGE GYPSIES. Camp of gypsies in house barges. Tell fortunes, sell potions, pay decent prices for stolen goods. One of the barges is a prison containing a knight that the gypsies are holding for ransom. Fortune teller holds an icon of Saint Ignatius.

MARSH WITCH. Witch sitting on front porch of webfooted hut, fishing and smoking a pipe. Loves smoked fish, blue cheese and fruitcake. The moon is in love with her and will control the tides at her command. Her beloved familiar is a black cat with a white spot on its chest, the second son of the King of Cats, which preys on the field mice.
A *circle of [3d6] stones* stands here.
(if by the sea) Hidden harbor. Base of a luckless pirate captain.
The remains of an old fishing settlement. A strange statue, clearly dredged from the ocean floor, is located in the center of town - surrounded by corpses.
Marsh lands. *Very difficult terrain* to cross. No potable water.
Salt water marsh as far as the eye can see. Long legged birds prey on fish in the shallows.
Atop a flat-topped hill is the decapitated head of a statue, some fourteen feet tall on its side. It depicts the face of a gaping fish-man.
Blasted craggy hills that have been sculpted by the NE wind into the shapes sleeping woman.
The burial mounds here hold the most honored dead of local nomadic tribes. One of the mounds has recently been broken into or out of, whatever was inside is gone.

These plains contain several cairns spread in a circular formation over a 4 mile radius. At the center is a very large barrow with a low entry. Deep bellow the barrow lies a helmet of incredible powers.
Corpse of a hill giant ripped apart by wild dogs. Has [10d50]gp, 2-handed sword (a sword to him), and clear footprints leading from the [direction].
A city amidst swamps, that blocks the safest route through the treacherous ground. It takes a tax of 5% on any valuables one wants to bring through it, not just trade goods.
Ghouls live under the ancient battlefield here. The ghoul king wields the sword of an ancient prince who died on the battlefield.
This expanse gets rockier to the [direction], and the shore is one large flat stone expanse. Huge Lizards come here to bask in the sun and drink the water.
Old meteor impact crater. Lucky prospectors might find meteor splinters.

(if by the sea) A dwarf and three halfling merchants are picking through shipwreck remains washed ashore. Their orcish servants wait chained and ready to pull two surviving carts. If questioned they reveal they are from a shipping town in the mountains of [location theme] and were returning from trading with alchemists.
Pale Worms. Lots of pale worms here.

THE DROWNING BELL. A massive bronze bell suspended from a rotting wooden frame in the middle of a mire. It rings once for every soul that has drowned within a mile. If struck, it echoes with the screams of the dead, momentarily stunning all within earshot.

WHISPERING REEDS. A patch of tall, indigo reeds that capture the last words of anyone who dies in the swamp. By sitting quietly, one can hear secrets, warnings, or the location of hidden caches, but the reeds also draw the attention of hungry will-o'-the-wisps.

THE MOSS-CHOKED GALLEON. An ancient warship resting far away from the nearest ocean, its hull overgrown with glowing, bioluminescent moss. It serves as a neutral ground for swamp-dwelling tribes to trade, though the ship's hold is whispered to contain a restless spectral captain.

SHRINE OF THE BLEACHED SKULL. A small stone altar topped with a massive, bleached dragon skull. Offerings of fresh meat left here are gone by morning, replaced by 1d6 silver coins or a minor piece of useful gossip about the local terrain.
THE BUBBLING CAULDRON. A naturally occurring hot spring that smells of sulfur and old leather. Swimming in it heals 1d8 HP but leaves the skin a permanent, faint shade of green.
THE LEAPER'S LOG. A fallen titanic tree spanning a bottomless sludge-pit. It is the only way across, but a territorial giant frog demands a 'song of great sorrow' or a shiny bauble as a toll.

THE HANGING GARDENS OF GRIMREED. Dozens of wicker cages hanging from the branches of a Great Willow, each containing a different species of rare, carnivorous orchids. A local herbalist offers a reward for any orchid that hasn't eaten in a week.

THE CINDER-WITCH'S CHIMNEY. A tall, jagged rock formation that constantly vents thick, grey smoke. It marks the entrance to a network of volcanic tunnels beneath the swamp where fire-resistant goblins forge obsidian blades.

THE GRAVEYARD OF GIANTS. A valley of massive, petrified ribs rising from the muck. It is a holy site for local lizardfolk, who believe the bones belong to the ancestors of the world. They do not take kindly to outsiders 'prodding the relics.'

THE TURTLE'S BACK TAVERN. A small shack built on the shell of an enormous, slow-moving snapping turtle. It serves 'Swamp Grog' (Con save or blinded for 1d4 hours) and stays in one place for only a few days before the turtle migrates.
THE MIRROR MUD. A perfectly circular patch of silver-colored mud that reflects the sky perfectly, even at midday. Stepping into it transports the traveler to a random location elsewhere in the swamp.

THE WEAVING SPIDERS' CLOISTER. A cluster of trees completely encased in thick, silvery webbing. A group of reclusive monks lives within, spinning 'spirit silk' that can bind ghosts. They trade for rare insects or fine salt.

THE ROTTING CATHEDRAL. A massive, derelict structure made entirely of petrified peat and bone. It is the lair of a 'Swamp Oracle'—a three-headed vulture that speaks in riddles and demands 'history' (a told story) as payment.

THE SINKING STATUE. A marble statue of a weeping knight that sinks one inch deeper into the mire every year. It is currently waist-deep. Legend says when it fully disappears, the swamp will swallow the nearest city.
THE OIL-SLICK SPRINGS. A series of dark pools covered in a thick, iridescent oil. Local scouts use the oil to waterproof their gear, but it is highly flammable and draws fire-spitting beetles.

THE FLOATING APIARY. Dozens of hollowed-out gourds tied to buoys, filled with 'Cinder-Bees.' Their honey is spicy and grants resistance to cold for 1 hour, but the bees are aggressive and can track a thief by their scent for miles.

THE BONE-STITCHER'S WORKSHOP. A small hut made of woven vines and ribs. An eccentric hermit lives here, offering to 'mend' broken equipment using magically hardened bone for a small fee of 'teeth' (any kind).
THE WHIRLING MIRE. A slow-moving whirlpool of thick, black mud. It periodically spits out artifacts from a lost civilization, but anything caught in its center is crushed by the pressure.

THE SALT-LICK OBELISK. A single, jagged pillar of pure rock salt. It draws all manner of swamp wildlife, including a pair of extremely defensive owlbears that treat the obelisk as their personal salt-lick.
plains: A [large|gigantic|diligent] burrowing [creature|animal|monster] has disturbed the [rich|dry|dark|root-bound] [earth|soil|clay|loam] here.
The [sandy|rocky|pebble-strewn] [slopes|hillsides|valleys] are heavily eroded by [wind and rain|recent flooding|frequent foot-traffic].
[Frequently|Carefully|Meticulously|Recently] [plowed|tilled|planted] [terraces|slopes] make the most use of [fertile|limited flat|unforgiving] land here.

Atop a [large|small|squat|surprisingly uniform|very rotund] [hill|hummock|mound|promontory|slope|tor|talus|knoll] is a [ring|arrangement|circle|jutting pile] of [standing|granite|marble|hewn|crudely worked] [stones|boulders|megaliths].
[Gentle|Rising|Steep] slopes [reveal|cradle|contain] an orchard of [wild|carefully pruned|sagging] [Apple|Lemon|Orange|Cherry|Peach|Plum|Fig] trees.
A small [brook|creek|stream] meanders [between|through|down] the [hills|hummocks|slopes|tors|knolls|hillocks] here.

[Celebrating|Memorializing|Immortalizing|Commemorating|Eulogizing|Demonizing] [an ancient battle|a crushing defeat|a long dead monarch], a [weathered|eroded|nearly demolished] statue graces a nearby [hill|hummock|mound|promontory|slope|tor|talus|knoll|crest].
The [hills|hummocks|slopes|tors|knolls|hillocks] here [appear|are|seem] unnatural, possibly ancient barrows of some kind.
[Brilliant|Innumerable|Blossoming] [crimson|violet|golden|indigo|pale white|saffron|azure] flowers coat the [hills|hummocks|slopes|tors|knolls|hillocks] here like thick, wet paint.

[Lined|Encircled|Fenced] with [small boulders|decaying wooden slats|cultivated hedges] is what appears to be a[n overgrown| carefully tended| forgotten] [cemetery|graveyard|garden] for [halflings|gnomes].
The [ruts|furrows|gouges] of [centuries of|years of|recent] [wagon|cart|coach] wheels form a [friendly|uneven|muddy] path here.
Someone has nailed a [battered shield|tender love letter|sun-faded wanted poster|wooden holy symbol] to a [sickly|large|ancient] [Oak|Pine|Elm|Birch|Willow|Ash|Yew] tree.
A [small|large|animal|foul-smelling] [den|burrow] of some kind is [carefully|clumsily|barely] concealed [with brush|by bushes|behind some stones].
Remnants of a [recent|large|sacred] bonfire and [celebration|ceremony|religious sacrifice] dot the [hills|hummocks|slopes|tors|knolls|hillocks|hilltops].
A[ burned| ransacked|n abandoned| charred] [wagon|barn|farmstead] lies ahead, eerily [silent|peaceful|foreboding] in the [growing|soft|whispering] [winds|breeze].
Crumbling with [age|erosion|disrepair], the [ruins|remains|supports|stony legs] of an ancient aqueduct [wind and hop|snake|straddle the valleys] over hillsides.
Here the [hills|hummocks|slopes|tors|knolls|hillocks|hilltops] grow [rocky|craggy|rough|jagged|pebbly], bearing only a few tufts of [Knifebloom|Ironweed|Briar|Thornwort] grass.

On the side of a large [rise|crest|cliff|hillock|slope], crushed [chalk|glittering gypsum|crimson clay|glimmering pyrite] forms the image of [a stylized ram skull|a rearing horse|a sleeping dragon|a hunter with a spear|an unblinking eye|the symbol of [Fate|War|Harvest|Death|the Storm]].
[Unnervingly|Surprisingly|Noticeably] uniform, the [hills|hummocks|slopes|tors|knolls|hillocks|hilltops] here resemble [giant tortoise shells|hay-bales|balding heads].
The sun-bleached bones of [thousands of|hundreds of|several|a dozen] [horses|antelope|bison|sheep|goats] [fill|dot|blanket] the [dale|gorge|valley|basin|glen|dell|vale|swale|coulee|lowland] here.
